title: [ipv6_addr.c:32-34] Host-side LOG_INF/LOG_DBG are silently swallowed
description: At lines 32-34, the host-side fallback macros for LOG_INF and LOG_DBG expand to ((void)0), silently swallowing all arguments. This is fine for production but during development/debugging, important information is lost. Consider: at minimum, LOG_WRN/LOG_ERR go to stderr which is good, but LOG_INF includes 'Link-local: %s' at line 307 which would be useful during host testing. This is arguably by design (suppress info in tests per comment) but could surprise developers.
close_reason: Fixed: LOG_ERR/LOG_WRN now go to stderr on host, LICHEN_HOST_VERBOSE enables LOG_INF/LOG_DBG
